chunks.text: Berkeley EECS graduate programs consistently top national rankings, providing one of the best educational experiences anywhere. Our graduate students are immersed in an intellectually rigorous, interdisciplinary, globally aware environment, and have the opportunity to study and do research with faculty world-renowned for their innovation and discovery. We welcome your application to our competitive graduate programs. We look forward to the mixture of backgrounds and approaches that each new group of graduate students brings to us.
chunks.question: What characterizes Berkeley EECS graduate programs?

chunks.text: Berkeley EECS offers two degrees for planning a career in industry: Master of Engineering (MEng) and Five Year Master of Science (5th Yr M.S.). These are professional degrees designed for industry careers.
chunks.question: Which degrees does Berkeley EECS offer for industry careers?
chunks.answer: MEng and Five Year Master of Science.

chunks.text: Berkeley EECS offers three degrees for planning a career in industrial R&D and teaching: the Master of Science (M.S.), the Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.), and the combined M.S./Ph.D. The 2-year M.S. degree is intended primarily for registered UC Berkeley Ph.D. students who want to add the degree. Other students interested in a research-oriented degree should consider applying directly to the M.S./Ph.D. or Ph.D. program.
chunks.question: What are the research-oriented degrees at Berkeley EECS?

chunks.text: If you are planning to immediately join the engineering profession without pursuing a Ph.D., the Master of Engineering (M.Eng.) program is specifically designed as a professional master’s degree. This contrasts with research-oriented degrees like M.S./Ph.D. or Ph.D.
chunks.question: Which degree is for immediate engineering careers?

chunks.text: The student-run Equal Access Assistance (EAAA) program aims to ensure that all applicants to higher degree programs (M.S./Ph.D.) at Berkeley EECS have access to guidance on the application process. Participants receive feedback on statements, CV, and other materials from current or recent EECS graduates. This feedback is Berkeley- and admissions-focused, not grammar or formatting advice.
chunks.question: What does the EAAA program provide?

chunks.text: Participation in EAAA does not guarantee admission nor affect the admissions decision-making process in any way. Application for admission to Berkeley EECS must be completed separately from the EAAA program.
chunks.question: Does EAAA guarantee admission to Berkeley EECS?
chunks.answer: No, it does not guarantee admission.
